journal_entry.rationale: Inventory loss due to typhoon is recorded as an expense (Loss on Inventory - Casualty) and reduces the Inventory asset account. Under IFRS, casualty losses are recognized in profit or loss when the loss occurs. The debit to Loss on Inventory records the expense, while the credit reduces the Inventory asset by the cost of the destroyed goods. No tax implications as this is a loss event, not a taxable supply or purchase.

output.reason: Inventory destroyed = asset decrease. The inventory asset account is credited to remove the destroyed inventory from the books (the debit would go to a loss/expense account).
